headline: Blue Origin rocket explodes during test in setback for Jeff Bezos
summary: STORY: :: Courtney BlairThis uncrewed Blue Origin rocket had just ignited in its launchpad on Thursday when it suddenly exploded...erupting into a massive fireball, sending a towering plume of flames and smoke into the air.Jeff Bezos' space venture said it had experienced an “anomaly,” a term commonly used to describe a launch failure or explosion.In a post on X, the Amazon billionaire said all personnel were accounted for and that it was "too early to know the root cause" of the incident.NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said the agency would work with partners to support an investigation, adding that NASA would provide information on any impact to its Artemis and Moon Base programs.The incident is a major setback for Bezos' Blue Origin, as it seeks to narrow the gap with Elon Musk’s IPO-bound SpaceX.Both billionaire-run companies have been racing to help return people to the moon by designing the lunar landers NASA will use.Musk responded on X to a video of the Blue Origin explosion, saying: “Most unfortunate. Rockets are hard."

raw.headline: Blue Origin rocket explodes during test in setback for Jeff Bezos
raw.summary: STORY: :: Courtney BlairThis uncrewed Blue Origin rocket had just ignited in its launchpad on Thursday when it suddenly exploded...erupting into a massive fireball, sending a towering plume of flames and smoke into the air.Jeff Bezos' space venture said it had experienced an “anomaly,” a term commonly used to describe a launch failure or explosion.In a post on X, the Amazon billionaire said all personnel were accounted for and that it was "too early to know the root cause" of the incident.NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said the agency would work with partners to support an investigation, adding that NASA would provide information on any impact to its Artemis and Moon Base programs.The incident is a major setback for Bezos' Blue Origin, as it seeks to narrow the gap with Elon Musk’s IPO-bound SpaceX.Both billionaire-run companies have been racing to help return people to the moon by designing the lunar landers NASA will use.Musk responded on X to a video of the Blue Origin explosion, saying: “Most unfortunate. Rockets are hard."

headline: Dell rallies about 40% on strong Nvidia‑powered AI server demand
summary: Shares of Dell Technologies soared nearly 40% in premarket trading on Friday, after robust demand for its Nvidia-powered ‌AI servers prompted the company to raise its annual revenue ‌and profit forecasts.  Dell has benefited significantly from data center investments by Alphabet and Amazon, as well as price increases and improved supply chain management.  On Thursday, Dell ‌raised its annual revenue forecast ⁠to $165 billion to $169 billion from $138 billion to $142 billion, and said it now expects AI server revenue of about $60 ⁠billion in fiscal 2027, up from $50 billion.

Dell has benefited significantly from data center investments by Alphabet and Amazon, as well as price increases and improved supply chain management.  On Thursday, Dell ‌raised its annual revenue forecast ⁠to $165 billion to $169 billion from $138 billion to $142 billion, and said it now expects AI server revenue of about $60 ⁠billion in fiscal 2027, up from $50 billion.

headline: Major Studios Shave Film Slate Costs By 2.8% To Help Boost Profit By 36.8%

headline: Dell shares rocket on bullish forecast for AI demand
summary: STORY: :: DellDell shares rocketed higher in after-hours trade Thursday, jumping around 39%.The surge came after the computer maker lifted revenue and profit expectations.Dell says it is seeing soaring demand for AI-optimized servers used in data centers.It’s benefiting as tech giants like Alphabet and Amazon plan to spend over $700 billion on AI-related infrastructure this year.Dell now expects annual revenue of up to $169 billion - way up on the previous forecast of up to $142 billion.AI server revenue is expected to hit $60 billion for fiscal 2027, $10 billion up on previous expectations.The company has been adjusting prices constantly as strong global demand causes the cost of memory chips to soar.:: DellOn a call with analysts, Chief Operating Officer Jeff Clarke said he knew customers were feeling the pain.But he said there was little sign of inflationary pressure easing.One analyst told Reuters that Dell looked well placed to cope with the chip shortage, helped by its scale and strong relationships with suppliers.

raw.headline: Dell shares rocket on bullish forecast for AI demand
raw.summary: STORY: :: DellDell shares rocketed higher in after-hours trade Thursday, jumping around 39%.The surge came after the computer maker lifted revenue and profit expectations.Dell says it is seeing soaring demand for AI-optimized servers used in data centers.It’s benefiting as tech giants like Alphabet and Amazon plan to spend over $700 billion on AI-related infrastructure this year.Dell now expects annual revenue of up to $169 billion - way up on the previous forecast of up to $142 billion.AI server revenue is expected to hit $60 billion for fiscal 2027, $10 billion up on previous expectations.The company has been adjusting prices constantly as strong global demand causes the cost of memory chips to soar.:: DellOn a call with analysts, Chief Operating Officer Jeff Clarke said he knew customers were feeling the pain.But he said there was little sign of inflationary pressure easing.One analyst told Reuters that Dell looked well placed to cope with the chip shortage, helped by its scale and strong relationships with suppliers.
